reasons: EMERGENCY: diversity collapsed with frozen particles

turns.thought: [OBSERVE] Swarm metrics show extreme diversity collapse: normalized diversity 2.57e-7, swarm radius 0.000245, position std mean 8.5e-6. Velocity nearly zero: mean velocity norm 9.84e-5, 86.7% of particles have near-zero velocity (frozen). All particles clustered at similar fitness (mean 15299.95, best 15299.64, std 0.163). Improvement per iteration is negligible (delta 0.055, relative improvement 3.6e-6). State labeled 'diversity_collapse'. Landscape analysis indicates high ruggedness (0.8903), many basins, estimated basin radius 1.2, moderate deceptiveness (0.3956). No particles at boundaries. | [DIAGNOSE] Premature convergence to a local basin. The swarm has collapsed onto a single point and lost all exploratory capability. The root cause is that all particles converged prematurely to the same narrow valley, and 87% are frozen, preventing any escape. The landscape's rugged multi-basin nature suggests this is likely a local minimum or deceptive basin, not the global optimum. | [STRATEGIZE] To escape the current basin and restore diversity, use basin_hopping. This tool targets particles to jump to random positions within the estimated basin radius (1.2) from gBest, which are likely to land in neighboring basins. A ratio of 0.5 (half the swarm) will inject substantial diversity without being overly disruptive. Increasing inertia weight to 1.2 afterwards encourages continued exploration. This is more targeted than random restart (levy_flight) because it uses the estimated basin structure.
turns.observation:
Action 'basin_hopping' was applied. Basin-hopped 15 particles within radius 1.2. Best cost changed from 1.529964e+04 to 1.370317e+04 (delta=+1.596470e+03).

Now REFLECT: Was your diagnosis correct? Did the intervention produce the expected improvement? Update your understanding and decide the next step. turns.thought: [REFLECT] The intervention succeeded dramatically: best cost dropped from 15299.64 to 13703.17, a 10.4% improvement, indicating the swarm escaped a local basin and entered a much better region. This confirms the diagnosis of premature convergence to a local basin, and basin_hopping was the appropriate tool, as it used the estimated basin radius to jump particles to neighboring basins. The swarm now has higher inertia weight (1.2) and a restructured population, so it should be able to exploit this new basin effectively. | [REVISED_DIAGNOSE] The swarm had converged to a suboptimal local basin. Basin_hopping successfully relocated half the particles into a better basin, restoring diversity and enabling further progress. The swarm is now in a promising region, and no immediate intervention is needed.
